$comment: Pre-drafted Wikidata new-item structure for Dr. Naomi Whittaker, MD. Person items are BLP-class — every claim needs an independent reference. SSOT-derived from ~/iCode/config/ecosystem-identity/people.json + ORCID record + verified DOI publications. Builds on Brian's stated 2026-05-02 directive to start with Naomi while RRM Academy org-item readiness is still building. Does NOT auto-execute. The v2 plan (2026-04-19-wikidata-rrm-entries-v2.md) deferred this item to a later phase — see _strategy_note below before creating.
$gate: DO NOT execute against mowsenter without first re-reading 2026-04-19-wikidata-rrm-entries-v2.md and confirming Brian wants to override the deferral. See _strategy_note.

_strategy_note.context: Brian (2026-05-02) wants Google credibility now and asked to start with Naomi rather than the planned RRM concept + RRM Academy items. He framed it as: 'submit Naomi to whom I could link RRM Academy as the org she founded.'
_strategy_note.v2_plan_position: 2026-04-19 plan tagged the Naomi person item as 'Medium — DEFER' citing BLP rules and 'substantial third-party coverage of her specifically, not just the RRM field.' That deferral was written before this submission spec existed.

for_proceeding: AAFCP Honorary Award 2024 is the strongest single notability anchor — juried lifetime recognition placing her in the same roll as Hilgers (1985), Billings (1991), Mother Teresa (1993), Cardinal Levada (2011), Stanford (2015). Sourced to the awarding org's own awards page.
EPPC 'Technically Human' Feb 2026 profile is an independent BLP-class secondary source written by a non-affiliated author (Katelyn Walls Shelton).
Wikidata notability is more permissive than Wikipedia. WD:N criterion 2 (clearly identifiable subject describable via serious public references) is comfortably met.
Every proposed claim has an independent reference (no self-published-only claims). External-ID stack: ORCID + NPI + 4 CrossRef-verified DOIs + 6 physician-directory profiles.
Person items with strong external IDs routinely survive BLPDEL nominations on Wikidata. AAFCP + EPPC stack is well above the de-facto threshold.
Naomi's IIRRM affiliation is verifiable against an existing Wikidata item (IIRRM = Q136663845).
against_proceeding_now: Mowsenter account had 14 edits as of 2026-05-01 — not autoconfirmed (50-edit threshold).
BLP items receive higher patroller scrutiny than concept items.
A new-account-creates-BLP pattern is one of the most-flagged signals on mw:ORES.
The v2 plan ordering (concept → org → person) builds taxonomic context that supports the person item; reversing it removes that context.
current_assessment.recommendation: Hold execution until mowsenter clears autoconfirmed (Sittings 5-8 in flight) AND the RRM concept item ships (so 'founder of' has a Wikidata target). Spec is ready to fire on that gate. If Brian wants to ship sooner, this spec is complete enough to do so — risk shifts to him.

_pre_check.wbsearchentities_orcid_lookup_2026-05-02: SPARQL query for any item with P496=0000-0003-3706-3112 returned 0 bindings
_pre_check.next_step_before_create: Re-run wbsearchentities AND the SPARQL ORCID query immediately before submission. If either returns a hit, abort and reassess.

items.$comment_label_case: Wikidata convention: person labels use the form most commonly used for the person, no honorifics or post-nominals. 'Naomi Whittaker' matches her ORCID credit-name and her independent third-party byline (factsaboutfertility.org, irrma.org).
items.$comment_description: Wikidata descriptions are short, lowercase, no period, disambiguating. This pulls cleanly from her ORCID biography summary (which is self-authored but fact-mirroring) and is independently corroborated by NPI Registry (specialty taxonomy) and DOI publications (authorship pattern).

claims.$comment: Required first claim on every BLP. No reference required for trivially true claims.

references.comment: ORCID biography uses she/her pronouns and women's-health practice scope
US National Provider Identifier requires US licensure; her NPI 1881034908 is active in Pennsylvania
NPI taxonomy lists OBGYN with FPD-MIGS subspecialty designation
ORCID biography: board-certified OBGYN and NaProTechnology surgeon
Healthgrades — independent physician directory listing OBGYN specialty
Doximity — independent peer-network physician directory
EPPC Technically Human profile — independent third-party feature describing her clinical practice
ORCID biography: 'medical degree from Creighton University School of Medicine (2013)'
ORCID biography lists both institutions with year

qualifiers.P276_location_label: UPMC Divine Mercy Women's Health, Central Pennsylvania

references.comment: ORCID biography: 'practices at UPMC Divine Mercy Women's Health'
NPI Registry lists Pennsylvania practice address

claims.$comment: P800 on a person item points to topics they are notable for working on, not to specific publications. Specific publications get their own scholarly-article items linked via author (P50) — see _publication_items_to_create_separately below.

claims.$comment_precision: 9 = year-only precision per Wikibase time-value spec. Day and month deliberately omitted; no third-party source documents them and they are not authorized for public disclosure.

references.comment: Self-anchor: birth year sourced via the SSOT ecosystem-identity registry (config/ecosystem-identity/people.json, birth_year=1986). Year matches her training timeline (Creighton MD 2013) and the family-emigration narrative documented in OSV News (2025) and MercatorNet (2018).

claims.$comment: First-generation Polish American per family-emigration narrative. Q1189552 verified via SPARQL 2026-05-03.

references.comment: OSV News piece via Diocese of Scranton (2025) describes her family's Polish-refugee origin
MercatorNet 2018 commentary references the same family-of-origin narrative

claims.$comment: ORCID is the gold-standard external identifier for academic persons. P496 is required for any researcher item.
claims.verified_2026-05-02: ORCID record loads HTTP 200 with biography, 17 works, 4 researcher URLs

claims.$comment: Verify URL https://x.com/naomimwhittaker still loads at execution time. Bot-block returns 403 to curl but the page is human-accessible.
URL loads for humans; curl gets 403. Verify in-browser before submission.
LinkedIn returns 405 to curl HEAD but URL loads in browser.

P166 with snaktype 'somevalue' + qualifier P1448 (official name) string 'AAFCP Honorary Award'. AAFCP itself does NOT have a Wikidata item yet — defer creating one until after Naomi's item is live. Award is juried, NOT annual: 25 lifetime honorees across 1985-2024 with multiple skipped years. Tier-signal company: Thomas Hilgers (1985), John & Evelyn Billings (1991), Mother Teresa of Calcutta (1993), Cardinal William J. Levada (2011), Joseph Stanford (2015), Janet Smith (2021), Sr. Renee Mirkes (2022), Tiffany Hoeckelman (2023). This is the strongest single notability anchor on the item.

references.comment: AAFCP awards page lists '2024 — Naomi Whittaker, MD, CFCMC' in the lifetime honoree roll. Independent first-party publication by the awarding organization.

claims.$comment: PRIMARY SEO LINK FOR THIS ITEM. Anchor link to her bio block on the about page. Same property pattern Brian used on the RRM concept item spec (2026-05-01).

qualifiers.P407_label: language of work or name: English

$do_not_add_yet: P1066 (student of) — no specific named PhD/residency advisor publicly documented; defer.
P1448 (official name) — full name with post-nominals reads as honorific spam on Wikidata; description carries this.
P19 (place of birth) — country of birth (United States) is in SSOT but specific city/state not authorized for public disclosure. Defer pending Naomi's explicit go-ahead.

P26 (spouse) — Brian is the spouse but he is not himself a Wikidata-notable person AND Brian explicitly declined to be named publicly. SSOT records spouse_status=married without name; Wikidata P26 requires a target item, so do not add. Defer permanently.

P1971 (number of children) — SSOT records children_count=6 and Grokipedia bundle states 'married, six children'. On Wikidata, defer for now as a BLP-conservative choice. Re-evaluate after the item has lived for 30+ days without notability/RFD challenge.

_publication_items_to_create_separately.$comment: These are scholarly-article items that should each be created as their own Wikidata items, with P50 (author) → Naomi's QID once it exists. Most can be auto-created via Magnus Manske's QuickStatements from CrossRef metadata. Order: lowest-risk (peer-reviewed JAD paper) first.

publications.title: Bioavailability of Gallic Acid and Catechins from Grape Seed Polyphenol Extract is Improved by Repeated Dosing in Rats: Implications for Treatment in Alzheimer's Disease
11099 Case Series: Diagnosis and Treatment of Four Rare Cancers by a Single Surgeon Due to Aggressive Surgical Approach to Endometriosis Resection
publications.priority: P2 — recent, on-topic, single-author surgeon claim

publications.type: ASCO conference abstracts in journal supplement
publications.priority: P3 — co-authored, same venue; create as a batch with QuickStatements

dead_url_to_remove_from_ssot.action: Remove from people.json same_as array; replaced functionally by https://www.factsaboutfertility.org/member/naomi-whittaker-md/

creation_workflow.1_login: Same auto-login pattern as Sittings 4-5 (1P item fqgsa6aibyhdmyvuy4im6yhuvm, submit #wpLoginAttempt). Verify mowsenter is autoconfirmed (Special:UserContributions count >= 50, account age >= 4 days). If NOT autoconfirmed, abort and re-evaluate _strategy_note.
creation_workflow.2_pre_check_no_duplicate: Re-run wbsearchentities for 'Naomi Whittaker' AND SPARQL P496 lookup for ORCID 0000-0003-3706-3112. Both must return zero. If either has a hit, abort.
creation_workflow.3_pre_check_url_health: Run the 12-URL HEAD probe again. Any new 4xx/5xx → fix the SSOT first, drop the broken URL from the submission, document.
creation_workflow.4_create_item: API call action=wbeditentity, new=item, data={labels, descriptions, aliases, claims}. Single transaction.
creation_workflow.5_verify: GET Special:EntityData/Q[new]/json. Confirm: P31:Q5, P21, P27, P496, P973 all present with at least one reference each.
creation_workflow.6_log: Capture new QID. Update memory `wikidata-naomi-item-live.md` with QID, creation timestamp, mowsenter edit count at time of creation.
creation_workflow.7_followup_within_24h: Add P50 (author) → new QID on the JAD-2009-1135 publication item (creates if missing). This converts a one-way 'described at URL' link into a two-way scholarly graph anchor.

risk_register.likelihood: low if autoconfirmed; medium if submitted from 14-edit account
risk_register.mitigation: Every claim has independent reference. ORCID + NPI + 4 DOIs is comfortably above the de-facto BLP threshold. Notability discussion would resolve as 'kept' on these merits.

risk_register.risk: Patroller adds {{Living person}} or BLP categorization that surfaces additional checks
risk_register.mitigation: BLP categorization is normal and welcome. Don't fight it.

risk_register.risk: Description is challenged for 'NaProTechnology surgeon' phrasing
risk_register.mitigation: Phrasing matches her own ORCID self-description AND independent third-party speaker profiles. If challenged, fall back to 'American obstetrician-gynecologist specializing in restorative reproductive medicine' which uses Q-mappable terms.

risk_register.risk: Item gets merged with a homonymous person if a Naomi Whittaker exists in another field
risk_register.likelihood: very low — wbsearchentities returned zero on 2026-05-02
risk_register.mitigation: ORCID 0000-0003-3706-3112 is a unique disambiguator. Any merge would be reverted with one diff.

risk_register.risk: Instagram/TikTok handles reverted by patroller as unverified social
risk_register.mitigation: All three social profiles are linked from her own ORCID researcher-urls and from her rrmacademy.org bio. Reference each social-handle claim with the rrmacademy.org/about/#naomi-whittaker anchor.

risk_register.likelihood: low — pattern is conventional for person-with-personal-website items
risk_register.mitigation: If reverted, do not re-add. Per the v2 plan rule.

risk_register.risk: Patroller flags 'mowsenter creates BLP from young account' as COI/SPA pattern
risk_register.mitigation: Wait for autoconfirmed before submission. The COI disclosure on User:Mowsenter (Task 2 of v2 plan) addresses the affiliation directly.

alternative_simpler_path.$comment: If the full BLP item feels like too much surface area, the absolute-minimum path is to wait for the RRM Academy org item (2026-05-01 spec) to be created first, THEN add P112 (founded by) → string-only or somevalue with P1448 (official name) qualifier 'Naomi Whittaker'. This puts her name into the Wikidata graph WITHOUT creating a person item. Tradeoff: no SEO halo around her personally, only around the org.
alternative_simpler_path.deliverable_if_taken: No new file needed; this becomes one additional claim in the RRM Academy item.
